_comment: Machine-readable drift ledger for the ChravelApp drift-control audit. Human narrative + source-of-truth matrix live in DRIFT_AUDIT.md. Classifications: CONFIRMED_DRIFT | INTENTIONAL_VARIATION | STALE_ARTIFACT | DUPLICATE_AUTHORITY | MISSING_AUTHORITY | POTENTIAL_DRIFT | BLOCKED_EXTERNAL | FALSE_POSITIVE. Severity: P0..P3. Status: FIXED | GATED | QUARANTINED | DEFERRED.

findings.expected: revenuecat-webhook is a shared-secret webhook (auth via REVENUECAT_WEBHOOK_SECRET header) and must have [functions.revenuecat-webhook] verify_jwt = false, like stripe-webhook and stream-webhook.
findings.observed: No [functions.revenuecat-webhook] block exists, so a CLI deploy inherits verify_jwt = true; the Supabase gateway rejects RevenueCat's JWT-less calls before the function's own secret check runs.
findings.evidence: config.toml has no revenuecat-webhook block; function reads REVENUECAT_WEBHOOK_SECRET and timing-safe compares the Authorization header. Detected by scripts/drift/check-edge-function-parity.mjs.
findings.userImpact: iOS subscription entitlement webhooks can silently fail when deployed via CLI, causing paid-user entitlement drift.
findings.securityImpact: None from this change (adds a documented public gate for an endpoint that already enforces a shared secret).
findings.likelyOrigin: Function added without a matching config.toml block (config.toml is a protected file; deploys may have gone through Lovable/dashboard where the gate was set out-of-band).
findings.remediation: Add [functions.revenuecat-webhook] with verify_jwt = false to supabase/config.toml (protected file — human change).
findings.prevention: scripts/drift/check-edge-function-parity.mjs (npm run drift:functions) fails on any NEW webhook-intent function lacking verify_jwt = false.

findings.expected: Every [functions.NAME] block has a corresponding supabase/functions/NAME/ code directory.
findings.observed: 5 config blocks reference functions with no code in this repo: approve-join-request, batch-generate-embeddings, gemini-voice-proxy, gemini-voice-session, livekit-token.
findings.evidence: comm of config block names vs function dirs. Detected by scripts/drift/check-edge-function-parity.mjs.
findings.userImpact: None directly; indicates either deleted functions whose gate config lingers, or functions deployed from another source.
findings.securityImpact: Low — stale public gates (4 of the 5 are verify_jwt=false) for non-existent code.
findings.likelyOrigin: Functions removed/renamed without cleaning config.toml, or deployed outside this repo (Lovable).
findings.remediation: For each: remove the config block if the function is retired, or restore the function code. Requires a human decision + config.toml edit.
findings.prevention: check-edge-function-parity.mjs fails on any NEW orphaned block; the existing 5 are quarantined in edge-function-parity-baseline.json.

findings.expected: Each function's JWT posture is explicit and intentional.
findings.observed: 54 of 98 function directories have no config.toml block and silently inherit verify_jwt = true. Most are correct, but webhook/public-intent ones must be verified (revenuecat-webhook is the confirmed break; health, fetch-og-metadata, web-push-send, push-notifications, get-trip-detail warrant review).
findings.evidence: 54-dir set computed from config vs dirs. Only revenuecat-webhook trips the webhook-secret/-webhook heuristic.
findings.userImpact: Public/webhook endpoints that need anonymous access may be gated (as in DRIFT-01).
findings.securityImpact: Neutral-to-positive (default is the safe direction); risk is availability, not exposure.
findings.likelyOrigin: config.toml only lists a subset of functions; the rest rely on the platform default.
findings.remediation: Human review of the public-intent subset; do NOT flip verify_jwt without tracing callers. No automated change made.
findings.prevention: check-edge-function-parity.mjs inventories all 54 and flags webhook-intent gaps; extend the heuristic as new public endpoints appear.

findings.expected: Each migration has a unique 14-digit timestamp prefix so apply order is deterministic.
findings.observed: 23 timestamp prefixes are shared by 2+ files (one prefix by 4 files); 22 filenames do not match YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_slug.sql (legacy 00x_, empty-slug, 8-digit).
findings.evidence: scripts/drift/check-migration-drift.mjs over 502 migration files; lint-migrations.ts already reports these as non-failing warnings.
findings.userImpact: A clean bootstrap can apply colliding migrations in a different order than production did, so environments can diverge.
findings.securityImpact: Indirect (RLS/policy migrations applying out of order).
findings.likelyOrigin: Parallel branches/agents choosing the same or hand-picked timestamps; Lovable UUID-slug migrations.
findings.remediation: Do NOT rename applied migrations. Existing collisions are grandfathered in migration-drift-baseline.json; new collisions are now blocked.
findings.prevention: scripts/drift/check-migration-drift.mjs (npm run drift:migrations) fails on any NEW duplicate timestamp or naming violation.

findings.category: Tooling: fragmented drift checks / missing CI coverage
findings.expected: One unified, discoverable drift-check entry point wired into CI.
findings.observed: Checks were fragmented: stream parity and IAP parity ran in NO workflow; schema-drift/env-coverage/lint-migrations had no npm alias (CI invoked them via raw npx tsx); permission drift lived in its own workflow; no npm run drift:check existed.
findings.userImpact: Drift categories could merge without a gate; contributors had no single command.
findings.securityImpact: Parity checks (auth-sensitive) not consistently enforced pre-merge.
findings.likelyOrigin: Checks added incrementally by different agents without consolidation.
findings.remediation: Added scripts/drift-check.mjs (npm run drift:check) orchestrating 7 local checks + 2 external (skipped when creds absent); added .github/workflows/drift-check.yml; added drift:* npm aliases.

findings.expected: types.ts has a DO-NOT-EDIT banner and a wired regeneration command.
findings.observed: No banner; regen command only documented in prose. Column-reference drift is caught by check-schema-drift.ts (in CI), but full-schema regen relied on developer discipline.
findings.evidence: types.ts line 1 is `export type Json`; no package.json regen script existed.
findings.userImpact: Stale types can hide schema changes from the type checker.
findings.likelyOrigin: Generated file checked in without generator wiring.
findings.remediation: Added `db:types` npm script (supabase gen types --linked). Banner deferred (the generator would strip a hand-added banner).
findings.prevention: check-schema-drift.ts remains the column-level gate; db:types documents the canonical regen path.

findings.expected: Every Deno.env.get('VAR') is documented as a parseable key in an env example.
findings.observed: VERTEX_PROJECT_ID and VERTEX_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY are used in fcmV1.ts but appear in the example files only as prose comments, so check-env-coverage --strict fails on them (CI runs it non-strict, so it passes today).
findings.evidence: npx tsx scripts/check-env-coverage.ts --strict exits 1 on these two.
findings.userImpact: None at runtime; blocks tightening env coverage to --strict.
findings.likelyOrigin: Vars documented descriptively rather than as KEY= entries.
findings.remediation: Document the two vars as parseable keys in .env.production.example, then promote drift:env-coverage to --strict. Deferred to avoid mis-signaling the env contract without owner confirmation.
findings.prevention: drift:check runs env-coverage (non-strict, matching CI); follow-up promotes to --strict.

findings.expected: All trip-scoped query keys come from the tripKeys factory so invalidation is consistent.
findings.observed: tripKeys is used in ~42 files but many hooks hand-write inline keys that duplicate/bypass it (e.g. useTripTasks.ts inline ['tripTasks', tripId, isDemoMode] duplicates tripKeys.tasks; usePendingActions, useProTrips, joinRequestMutations, featureFlags, etc.). Only the notifications slice has an ownership guard.
findings.evidence: Grep of inline array keys vs tripKeys usage.
findings.userImpact: Invalidation can update some screens but not others (stale UI after mutations/realtime).
findings.likelyOrigin: Factory introduced after many hooks already used inline keys; no enforcement.
findings.remediation: Extend tripKeys to cover the missing slices and migrate hooks; add a query-key ownership lint. Deferred — touches ~dozen src/** hooks and needs per-hook care (higher regression risk than this control-only PR).
findings.prevention: Follow-up: generalize scripts/qa/store-query-ownership-guard.cjs into a factory-enforcement lint.

findings.expected: Components go through hooks/services, not direct Supabase calls; lint:dal enforces the DAL boundary.
findings.observed: lint:dal only guards two edge endpoints (artifact-search, enhanced-ai-parser). Direct supabase.from/functions.invoke appears ~11 times across ~8 component files (CreateTripModal, ConsumerBillingSection, OutstandingPayments, TravelCompanySection, …).
findings.evidence: Grep for supabase.from( / functions.invoke( under src/components.
findings.userImpact: Inconsistent error/retry/auth handling; bypasses the canonical DAL retry envelope.
findings.securityImpact: Low-medium (ad-hoc queries may not follow the standard membership/error patterns).
findings.likelyOrigin: DAL introduced narrowly; broad enforcement never added.
findings.remediation: Broaden the DAL guard (or an ESLint no-restricted-syntax rule) to flag direct Supabase calls in src/components. Deferred — needs a triage of legitimate exceptions to avoid breaking the build.
findings.prevention: Follow-up lint rule; drift:check would include it once green.

findings.category: D.69 test-suite drift (validator test never runs)
findings.expected: The validate-env unit test executes in CI.
findings.observed: vitest include globs (src/**, supabase/functions/**) exclude scripts/**, so scripts/__tests__/validate-env.test.ts never runs (per TEST_GAPS.md).
findings.userImpact: Env validator regressions go uncaught by the test suite.
findings.likelyOrigin: Test added under scripts/ which is outside the vitest include set.
findings.remediation: Add a scripts test project/glob to vitest, or run it via a dedicated script. Deferred (vitest config change; verify no perf/OOM impact on the sharded suite).

findings.category: A.13 permission-matrix SQL output not drift-checked
findings.expected: All three generated artifacts (2 .ts + 1 .sql) are checked for staleness.
findings.observed: check-permission-matrix-drift.mjs re-checks only the two .ts files for staleness, not the generated .sql RLS function.
findings.userImpact: A stale generated SQL function could diverge from the matrix used by the frontend/edge.
findings.securityImpact: Medium (RLS authorization derived from the matrix).
findings.likelyOrigin: SQL output added to the generator after the drift checker was written.
findings.remediation: Extend check-permission-matrix-drift.mjs to also fail if the .sql regenerates differently. Deferred (small, but modifies an auth-sensitive checker; warrants focused review).
